goal: Land Serena-hover enrichment for the G1 structural group (find_definition / find_references / go_to_definition) as a dependency-inverted `SerenaHoverClient` seam + an `enrich_find_definition` fusion function in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs`, with the frozen acceptance test `executor::test_serena_g1_fusion` covering both the Serena-active and Serena-absent paths so `P1-W5-F02` flips to passes=true.
plan_summary: Follow the `fake_serena_client` precedent from `ucil-lsp-diagnostics::{call_hierarchy,quality_pipeline}` (established by the already-passing P1-W5-F06) and the DEC-0008 dependency-inversion pattern. Add a `SerenaHoverClient` async trait in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` (one method: `async fn hover(...) -> Result<Option<HoverDoc>, HoverFetchError>`), a `HoverDoc` + `HoverSource` pair, a `HoverFetchError` (#[non_exhaustive] thiserror enum), and a pure `enrich_find_definition` async function that takes a `SymbolResolution` + callers + an `Option<&impl SerenaHoverClient>` and returns an `EnrichedFindDefinition { resolution, callers, hover }`. Errors from the client become `hover: None` (best-effort enrichment, logged via `tracing::warn!`) so a Serena outage never breaks the G1 response. The test `test_serena_g1_fusion` lives at executor.rs module root per DEC-0005 and exercises three scripted scenarios against a `ScriptedFakeSerenaHoverClient`: (1) Serena returns hover markdown → `hover = Some(HoverDoc { source: HoverSource::Serena, .. })`; (2) client=None (Serena degraded) → `hover = None`; (3) client returns Err → `hover = None` + warn log (verified via `tracing_test`-free approach: just assert best-effort returns). No changes to `server.rs`, `handle_find_definition`, or the MCP JSON-RPC envelope in this WO — wiring the enrichment into the live handler is reserved for a follow-up WO because P1-W4-F05's frozen selector (`server::test_find_definition_tool`) already asserts the current response shape and adding the hover field would require an ADR + handler-signature change that exceeds F02's single-test scope.
scope_in: crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml — add `async-trait = { workspace = true }` under `[dependencies]` (workspace root already declares `async-trait = "0.1"` at line 103; no new workspace dep). Add under `[dev-dependencies]` if async-trait is only needed for the scripted fake; prefer `[dependencies]` because the trait itself is shipped.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs — add `pub use ucil_core::knowledge_graph::SymbolResolution;` if not already in scope (currently imported only inside `read_find_definition` in server.rs; executor.rs may need to re-import via `ucil_core::knowledge_graph::SymbolResolution`).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs — add `pub enum HoverSource { Serena, Lsp, None }` deriving `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash`, `#[non_exhaustive]`. Rustdoc cites §13.4 (diagnostics bridge sources) and DEC-0008 (Serena-managed vs degraded modes).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs — add `pub struct HoverDoc { pub markdown: String, pub source: HoverSource }` deriving `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq`. Rustdoc explains `markdown` is unprocessed LSP hover text (may include `## Signature`, ```` ``` ```` fences, etc.) and `source` tracks provenance so the MCP response can populate `_meta.source` precisely.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs — add `pub enum HoverFetchError { #[error("serena mcp channel error: {0}")] Channel(String), #[error("hover response decode failed: {0}")] Decode(String), #[error("hover request timed out after {0:?}")] Timeout(std::time::Duration) }` with `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]` and `#[non_exhaustive]`. String payloads (rather than concrete wrapped errors) keep this WO cycle-free from `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` or MCP-client internals; a later WO that implements the trait against a real MCP client can convert from its native errors via `.to_string()`.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs — add `#[async_trait::async_trait] pub trait SerenaHoverClient: Send + Sync { async fn hover(&self, resolution: &ucil_core::knowledge_graph::SymbolResolution) -> Result<Option<HoverDoc>, HoverFetchError>; }` — one method; returns `Ok(None)` if Serena has no hover info for the symbol; returns `Err(_)` on transport/decode/timeout failures. `Send + Sync` bounds so trait objects can live in `Arc<dyn SerenaHoverClient>`.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs — add `pub struct EnrichedFindDefinition { pub resolution: SymbolResolution, pub callers: Vec<Caller>, pub hover: Option<HoverDoc> }` deriving `Debug, Clone, PartialEq`. Where `pub struct Caller { pub qualified_name: Option<String>, pub file_path: String, pub start_line: Option<i64> }` deriving the same — mirrors the JSON shape `project_callers` in server.rs builds but promotes it to a typed struct so the fusion function is testable without JSON round-tripping.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs — add `pub async fn enrich_find_definition<C: SerenaHoverClient + ?Sized>(resolution: SymbolResolution, callers: Vec<Caller>, client: Option<&C>) -> EnrichedFindDefinition { match client { None => EnrichedFindDefinition { resolution, callers, hover: None }, Some(c) => { let hover = match c.hover(&resolution).await { Ok(h) => h, Err(e) => { tracing::warn!(symbol = ?resolution.qualified_name, error = ?e, "serena hover fetch failed; returning unenriched result"); None } }; EnrichedFindDefinition { resolution, callers, hover } } } }`. Instrument with `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.daemon.executor.enrich_find_definition", level = "debug", skip(client))]` per master-plan §15.2.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs — add a `#[cfg(test)] mod fake_serena_hover_client { ... }` sub-module containing `pub(super) struct ScriptedFakeSerenaHoverClient { by_qname: std::sync::Mutex<Vec<(String, Result<Option<HoverDoc>, HoverFetchError>)>> }` with `pub(super) fn new(...)` and `#[async_trait::async_trait] impl SerenaHoverClient for ScriptedFakeSerenaHoverClient`. Pattern intentionally mirrors `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs:677-750` (`fake_serena_client` module) — per DEC-0008 this is NOT a mock of Serena's MCP wire format; it implements UCIL's own `SerenaHoverClient` trait, the dependency-inversion seam. Lookup: find the first tuple whose `qname` matches `resolution.qualified_name.as_deref().unwrap_or("")` and return a CLONE of the response (`HoverFetchError` is not `Clone` by default — so the test either pre-clones by wrapping in `Arc<Result<...>>` OR the fake replaces each consumed entry with an empty `Ok(None)` to avoid the Clone requirement; executor can pick whichever is cleaner — prefer `Arc` since it matches the scripted-fake idiom in the lsp-diagnostics crate).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs — add `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test] async fn test_serena_g1_fusion()` AT MODULE ROOT (DEC-0005: no `mod tests {}` wrapper). The **frozen F02 selector** is `executor::test_serena_g1_fusion`. Test body covers three scripted scenarios (one test function; assertions serial, not parallel subtests — mirrors the multi-scenario pattern in `quality_pipeline::test_quality_pipeline_*` family):

* Scenario A — Serena ACTIVE returns hover: construct `SymbolResolution { qualified_name: Some("mymod::foo".into()), file_path: "src/lib.rs".into(), start_line: Some(42), .. }` via `SymbolResolution { ..Default::default() }` (or manual field init if Default isn't derived); build `ScriptedFakeSerenaHoverClient::new(vec![("mymod::foo".into(), Arc::new(Ok(Some(HoverDoc { markdown: "## foo

Returns bar".into(), source: HoverSource::Serena }))))])`; call `enrich_find_definition(resolution.clone(), vec![], Some(&client)).await`; assert `result.hover == Some(HoverDoc { markdown: "## foo

Returns bar".into(), source: HoverSource::Serena })`, `result.resolution == resolution`, `result.callers.is_empty()`.

* Scenario B — Serena absent (client = None): `enrich_find_definition(resolution.clone(), vec![Caller { qualified_name: Some("caller::one".into()), file_path: "src/caller.rs".into(), start_line: Some(10) }], None::<&ScriptedFakeSerenaHoverClient>).await`; assert `result.hover.is_none()`, `result.callers.len() == 1`, `result.resolution == resolution` (unchanged pass-through).

* Scenario C — Serena returns Err: script `("mymod::foo".into(), Arc::new(Err(HoverFetchError::Timeout(Duration::from_millis(500)))))`; call `enrich_find_definition(resolution, vec![], Some(&client)).await`; assert `result.hover.is_none()` (best-effort: errors suppressed, not propagated). Do NOT assert on tracing output — `tracing_test` is not a workspace dep and adding it is out of scope; the warn!() call is documented in the fusion function's rustdoc.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs — extend the existing `pub use executor::{ExecutorError, IngestPipeline, SOURCE_TOOL, TREE_SITTER_VALID_FROM};` line (line 79) to also re-export the new public surface: `pub use executor::{Caller, EnrichedFindDefinition, HoverDoc, HoverFetchError, HoverSource, SerenaHoverClient, enrich_find_definition};`. Alphabetise within each group to match the existing style (lines 82-102).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs — extend the module-level rustdoc block (lines 44-60) with a new paragraph describing the executor module's F02 surface: 'WO-0037 for `P1-W5-F02` extends [`executor`] with the [`executor::SerenaHoverClient`] dependency-inversion seam (DEC-0008) + [`executor::enrich_find_definition`] fusion function that enriches a [`executor::SymbolResolution`] with optional [`executor::HoverDoc`] context. Hover-fetch errors are logged and suppressed so a Serena outage never breaks the G1 response — wiring into [`server::McpServer::handle_find_definition`] is deferred to a later WO.' Match the existing prose tone (each module gets one paragraph; cross-module intra-doc links use the fully-qualified form to avoid the WO-0009/WO-0025 rustdoc ambiguity landmine).

Rustdoc on every new public item (`HoverSource`, `HoverDoc`, `HoverFetchError`, `SerenaHoverClient`, `Caller`, `EnrichedFindDefinition`, `enrich_find_definition`) — prose quality matching the existing executor.rs docs (see `ExecutorError` at lines 105-148 as the benchmark). Cite master-plan §13.4 (diagnostics bridge sources), §18 Phase 1 Week 5 (Serena fusion feature scope), DEC-0008 (MCP-channel semantic interpretation). Use fully-qualified paths for every intra-doc link — past landmine from WO-0009 / WO-0025 / WO-0024.
scope_out: NO wiring of `enrich_find_definition` into `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs::handle_find_definition`. The P1-W4-F05 acceptance selector (`server::test_find_definition_tool`) is frozen and asserts on the current `_meta` JSON shape; adding a `_meta.hover` field mid-stream would either (a) break that frozen test, or (b) require tripping guardrails to amend it. Wiring is a follow-up WO after the MCP envelope is officially extended — an ADR will accompany that step.

NO real `SerenaHoverClient` implementation against the live Serena MCP channel. The `PluginManager` already owns the Serena stdio pipe (WO-0010+); connecting `SerenaHoverClient` to that channel requires MCP `tools/call` plumbing + `initialize` handshake that exceeds single-WO scope. This WO ships ONLY the trait + fusion + scripted fake. An integration WO (e.g. WO-00XX-serena-hover-live) lands the real impl.

NO changes to `crates/ucil-core/**` — `SymbolResolution` is imported as-is from `ucil_core::knowledge_graph`. If a test needs `SymbolResolution { ..Default::default() }` and Default is not derived in core, construct manually via `SymbolResolution { id: None, qualified_name: None, file_path: "".into(), start_line: None, .. }` with explicit struct-update syntax — do NOT add a Default derive in core as that changes its public API.

NO changes to `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**` — the existing `SerenaClient` trait in that crate is for diagnostics / call-hierarchy / type-hierarchy. The new `SerenaHoverClient` in `ucil-daemon` is a sibling seam, not an extension of the lsp-diagnostics trait. If they converge later, an ADR consolidates them.
NO `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, `#[allow(dead_code)]` to suppress warnings on the trait, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `pass`-only test bodies — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.

NO `mocks/` or `MockSerenaHoverClient` generated by `mockall` / `mockito` / `wiremock` — the `ScriptedFakeSerenaHoverClient` is a hand-written scripted fake implementing the trait, NOT a generated mock. Root CLAUDE.md: mocking Serena MCP wire format is forbidden; hand-written fake of the local dependency-inversion trait is allowed (DEC-0008 precedent, already in use in lsp-diagnostics).

NO new ADR required — DEC-0008 already establishes the dependency-inversion pattern for Serena integration; this WO applies that pattern to hover. Raise a proposed ADR ONLY if implementation reveals a genuine spec ambiguity (e.g., hover markdown format, multi-hover cardinality).
NO adding `tracing-test`, `tracing-subscriber` test features, or similar telemetry-assertion crates. The warn!() log is observable via rustdoc contract only; no runtime assertion on log output.
NO performance benchmarks / criterion harness for fusion — the function is a trivial async merge; no perf target in master plan.

NO `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper around the test function — DEC-0005 mandates module-root flat tests. `mod fake_serena_hover_client` IS permissible as a sub-module for helper types because it holds non-test code (the fake itself is a reusable fixture), matching the lsp-diagnostics precedent.

NO changes to the existing `IngestPipeline`, `ExecutorError`, `SOURCE_TOOL`, `TREE_SITTER_VALID_FROM`, `language_from_extension`, or `kind_tag` items in executor.rs — F02 adds new public items; F04 items stay byte-identical.
acceptance: `pub use executor::{...}` in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` re-exports the new surface.
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon 'executor::test_serena_g1_fusion'` matches exactly 1 test, 1 passed — the F02 acceptance selector (frozen).
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon` — no regressions; existing executor/IngestPipeline/server/session/etc tests remain green.

`cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean (no new warnings; clippy::pedantic lints addressed — most common concerns for async trait objects: needless_pass_by_value, too_many_lines, module_name_repetitions).
`cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` exits 0 with no `^error` or `^warning: unresolved link` lines (rustdoc gate — past landmine from WO-0009 / WO-0024 / WO-0025).

Mutation check: `git stash` the body of `enrich_find_definition` (replace with `EnrichedFindDefinition { resolution, callers, hover: None }` regardless of client) → Scenario A assertion in `test_serena_g1_fusion` fails; `git stash pop` → test passes. (Verifier runs this per Phase-1 invariant + root CLAUDE.md `What done looks like` rubric.)
acceptance_criteria: cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon 'executor::test_serena_g1_fusion' --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tee /tmp/f02-WO-0037.log && grep -qE 'tests run: 1, 1 passed' /tmp/f02-WO-0037.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tee /tmp/daemon-WO-0037.log && ! grep -qE 'FAILED|test result: FAILED' /tmp/daemon-WO-0037.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee /tmp/clippy-WO-0037.log && ! grep -qE '^error' /tmp/clippy-WO-0037.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps 2>&1 | tee /tmp/doc-WO-0037.log && ! grep -qE '^(error|warning: unresolved)' /tmp/doc-WO-0037.log
context_refs: master-plan:§18 Phase 1 Week 5 lines 1762-1770 (Serena integration + G1 fusion feature scope)
master-plan:§3.2 rows 2-4 (G1 structural tools: find_definition, find_references, find_symbol)
rules:.claude/rules/rust-style.md (thiserror per-crate Error enum #[non_exhaustive]; no #[should_panic] for error paths; tokio::time::timeout on IO awaits; no mocks of Serena/LSP)
rules:.claude/rules/commit-style.md (~50 LOC per commit soft target; Conventional Commits with Phase/Feature/Work-order trailers)
decision:DEC-0008-lsp-bridge-via-serena-mcp-channel.md (dependency-inversion pattern for Serena integration; scripted-fake-trait-impl is the canonical test seam)
phase-log:ucil-build/phase-log/01-phase-1/CLAUDE.md (Phase 1 invariants; esp. #1 no mocks of Serena critical deps, #7 tokio::time::timeout)
predecessor:crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs:677-750 (`fake_serena_client` sub-module — ScriptedFakeSerenaClient trait-impl pattern this WO mirrors for hover)
predecessor:crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/quality_pipeline.rs:390-486 (same fake_serena_client pattern applied to the diagnostics pipeline — second reference implementation)

predecessor:ucil-build/work-orders/0032-treesitter-to-kg-pipeline.json (WO-0032 landed `IngestPipeline` + ExecutorError in executor.rs; this WO extends that file with a parallel Serena-fusion surface)

predecessor:ucil-build/work-orders/0033-find-definition-tool.json (WO-0033 landed the find_definition handler in server.rs; THIS WO DELIBERATELY DOES NOT TOUCH THAT HANDLER — wiring is deferred to a follow-up WO + ADR)

dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W5-F01: passes=true — Serena plugin manifest (plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml) + `ucil plugin install serena` path landed via WO-0013. `PluginManager` can spawn + health-check the Serena MCP stdio pipe; the seam this WO adds (`SerenaHoverClient`) is the bridge a future WO will glue to that pipe.
dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W4-F05: passes=true — `find_definition` handler in `server.rs:handle_find_definition` (WO-0033) resolves symbols via `KnowledgeGraph::resolve_symbol` and projects callers. `EnrichedFindDefinition` in this WO mirrors the handler's intermediate shape (resolution + callers) so the wiring WO can merge without refactoring the read path.

lessons_applied: DEC-0005 / WO-0006 / WO-0007 selector-mismatch avoidance: `test_serena_g1_fusion` lives at module root of executor.rs (matches the frozen F02 selector `executor::test_serena_g1_fusion` exactly); no `mod tests {}` wrapper; the `fake_serena_hover_client` sub-module holds NON-test fixture code only, matching the lsp-diagnostics precedent that already ships similar sub-modules without triggering DEC-0005.

WO-0009 / WO-0024 / WO-0025 rustdoc intra-doc link landmine: acceptance criterion #4 greps `cargo doc --no-deps` for `^error` or `^warning: unresolved`; rustdoc on new items uses fully-qualified intra-doc paths (`[`executor::SerenaHoverClient`]` not `[`SerenaHoverClient`]`) to avoid ambiguity.

WO-0012 / WO-0020 abandonment pattern: the frozen F02 selector `executor::test_serena_g1_fusion` is a SINGLE test whose green path does not depend on Docker, Serena binary, or any external service — executor/verifier loop cannot get stuck on environment absence; scripted fake is fully hermetic.

WO-0027 PATH-mutation race: this WO does NOT mutate process-wide state (PATH, CWD, env vars), tokio runtime config, or global signal handlers; the `async_trait` scripted fake is thread-safe via `Mutex<Vec<_>>` matching the lsp-diagnostics precedent. Executor must NOT copy the `std::env::set_var` pattern that bit WO-0027.

WO-0033 / WO-0034 / WO-0035 frozen-envelope lesson: the P1-W4-F05 `server::test_find_definition_tool` test asserts on the current `_meta` shape — adding a `_meta.hover` field would break it. Scope explicitly excludes `server.rs` edits for that reason; a follow-up WO lands the wiring + ADR-gated envelope update together.

Root CLAUDE.md `no mocks of Serena critical deps`: `ScriptedFakeSerenaHoverClient` is NOT a mock of Serena's MCP wire format — it implements UCIL's own `SerenaHoverClient` trait, the dependency-inversion seam per DEC-0008. The same pattern is already live for `SerenaClient` in lsp-diagnostics and passes verifier scrutiny (P1-W5-F06 flipped to passes=true).

WO-0010 / WO-0014 `#[non_exhaustive]` discipline: `HoverFetchError`, `HoverSource`, and the enum variants are `#[non_exhaustive]` so a later WO can add variants (e.g. `HoverFetchError::RateLimited`, `HoverSource::TreeSitter`) without a SemVer break.
